fields.title: pi: sandboxed bash/edit/write with approvals (asrt)
fields.description: Implement sandboxing as a *tau extension* (no pi-mono core changes). Override built-in tools by registering tools with same names (bash/edit/write) and intercept policy/approvals. Use anthropic-experimental/sandbox-runtime (ASRT) for OS-level sandboxing of bash. Provide /sandbox + /approval commands with blocking prompts and per-call rerun. Persist state via session custom entries. Targets macOS + Linux. Workspace root = git repo root else cwd. Allow git commits; deny writes to <workspace>/.git/hooks/** in non-danger modes.
fields.close_reason: All sandbox epic child tasks completed (ASRT bash/edit/write, approvals, diagnostics, missing deps handling, docs)

comments.author: ribelo
comments.text:
Grounding notes (pi-mono):
- bash tool: packages/coding-agent/src/core/tools/bash.ts (pluggable BashOperations) and core/bash-executor.ts + AgentSession.executeBash()
- edit/write tools: src/core/tools/edit.ts, write.ts (currently no policy checks; uses resolveToCwd in path-utils.ts)
- interactive UI supports blocking confirm w/ timeout via ctx.ui.confirm (see interactive-mode.ts showExtensionConfirm())
- extension wrapper can intercept tool_call/tool_result, and extensions can register commands (/...) (see extensions/loader.ts + agent-session _tryExecuteExtensionCommand)

Plan assumes implementing sandbox as a built-in inline extension factory loaded by default (so it can use ctx.ui.confirm and provide /sandbox + /approval).

comments.author: ribelo
comments.text:
Decisions locked:
- Defaults on first run: fs=workspace-write, net=deny, approval=ask, timeout=60s; write these defaults into user settings (~/.pi/settings.json) if unset.
- Sandbox applies only to model tool calls (bash/edit/write), not user-triggered !/!! bash.
- Network policy: deny | allowlist(domains, supports *.example.com) | allow-all. Approval policy governs whether to prompt to temporarily override net/fs for a single rerun.
- Session overrides should persist by writing a session custom entry (SessionManager supports type=custom entries).
- Approval prompt escalation (best-effort):
  * If fs=read-only and a write is attempted: prompt to rerun once with fs=workspace-write (net unchanged).
  * If fs=workspace-write and write outside workspace: prompt to rerun once with fs=danger-full-access.
  * If write hits <workspace>/.git/hooks/**: deny in non-danger modes, but when approval=ask, offer rerun once with fs=danger-full-access (explicit override).
  * If network denied (net=deny or allowlist miss): prompt to rerun once with net=allow-all (fs unchanged).
  * Headless/RPC: no prompt; deny.

comments.author: ribelo
comments.text: Clarification: tau is an extensions workspace. Do NOT create repo-local .pi/extensions. Extensions live under tau/extensions/* and are installed by symlinking/copying into ~/.pi/agent/extensions/<name>. Sandbox work will only touch tau/extensions/sandbox/**.
